package handler_test

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for tests_couple_to_gorm_model_for_api_contract_assertions.
// Rule intent: API tests that assert persistence model shapes instead of response contracts.

// GORM model — internal persistence shape
type User struct {
	gorm.Model
	Name      string    `gorm:"column:name" json:"name"`
	Email     string    `gorm:"column:email" json:"email"`
	Role      string    `gorm:"column:role" json:"role"`
	LastLogin time.Time `gorm:"column:last_login" json:"last_login"`
	IsActive  bool      `gorm:"column:is_active" json:"is_active"`
}

// API response DTO — what the client should actually see
type UserResponse struct {
	ID   uint   `json:"id"`
	Name string `json:"name"`
}

func GetUserHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	user := User{
		Model:     gorm.Model{ID: 1},
		Name:      "Alice",
		Email:     "alice@example.com",
		Role:      "admin",
		LastLogin: time.Now(),
		IsActive:  true,
	}
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, UserResponse{ID: user.ID, Name: user.Name})
}

func TestGetUserHandler_CouplesToGormModel(t *testing.T) {
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	c, _ := gin.CreateTestContext(w)

	c.Request = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/users/1", nil)
	GetUserHandler(c)

	// Anti-pattern: the test constructs a GORM model and asserts against its fields
	// instead of asserting against the API response DTO.
	expectedModel := User{
		Model:     gorm.Model{ID: 1},
		Name:      "Alice",
		Email:     "alice@example.com",
		Role:      "admin",
		LastLogin: time.Now(),
		IsActive:  true,
	}

	assert.Equal(t, expectedModel.Name, "Alice")
	assert.Equal(t, expectedModel.Email, "alice@example.com")
	assert.Equal(t, expectedModel.Role, "admin")
	assert.True(t, expectedModel.IsActive)
	assert.Equal(t, expectedModel.ID, uint(1))
}
